items.name: deploy-to-vercel
items.description: Deploy applications and websites to Vercel. Use when the user requests deployment actions like "deploy my app", "deploy and give me the link", "push this live", or "create a preview deployment".

items.name: ips-nosql
items.description: Provides full workflow coverage for IPS NoSQL Data Persistence (Cassandra/DynamoDB document store): entity schema XML authoring with PII/7216 classification, PR submission to dynamic-entities-release, QA/E2E/Performance/FMEA environment progression, production release, and Java Spring Boot SDK implementation (CRUD, batch, relationships, queries, auth, cross-region retry, anti-patterns).

items.name: argocd-k8s
items.description: Manages Argo CD deployments and Kubernetes operations at Intuit. Auto-triggers when user mentions deployment, deploy, argocd, argo, kubernetes, k8s, pods, rollout, sync, health status, degraded, progressing, suspended, promotion, rollback, environment status, qal, e2e, prf, stg, prd, usw2, use2, check deployment, monitor deployment, or deployment logs.

items.name: headless-orchestrator
items.description: How the standalone headless Intuit MCP orchestrator works — the always-running LaunchAgent (com.intuit.devx.headless-orchestrator) that replaces the IntuitDeveloperDesktopApp GUI and serves 127.0.0.1:53333 for all mcpkit-registered Intuit MCPs (ixp-mcp-server, nosql-remote-prd, subscription-mcp, dev-assist-remote-mcp, etc.). Use this whenever an Intuit MCP returns AuthenticationFailed / 401 / "connection refused" on 53333, when the user asks how the headless orchestrator authenticates or refreshes tokens, when auth needs a fresh device-code login (invalid_grant / refresh token expired), when the service needs restarting or status-checking, or when rebuilding the binary after pulling new source. It self-refreshes via a background scheduler (replacing the GUI's manual "refresh" button) and exposes dast_auth_status / dast_login MCP tools on /mcp/stream so an MCP client can inspect auth state and trigger an in-band re-login.

items.name: agent-browser
items.description: FALLBACK browser automation CLI. Use ONLY when Chrome DevTools MCP (browser-mcp-setup) cannot handle the task -- e.g., multi-page workflows with complex navigation, form automation with file uploads/drag-and-drop, tasks needing persistent browser state across sessions, or when Chrome DevTools MCP errors out. For standard browser testing, visual verification, and UI inspection, use browser-mcp-setup first.

items.name: hoppscotch-to-bruno
items.description: Convert Hoppscotch API collection JSON exports to Bruno format and import into Bruno app. Auto-triggers when user mentions hoppscotch, hopscotch, convert to bruno, import to bruno, hoppscotch export, api collection convert, hopp to bruno, migrate from hoppscotch.

items.name: intuit-docker-registry
items.description: Intuit Docker/Artifactory registry knowledge - authentication, building, pushing images, registry endpoints, troubleshooting. Auto-triggers when user mentions docker registry, artifactory, docker login, docker push, docker pull, docker.intuit.com, container registry, image push, registry credentials, artifactory token, local build and push, docker build intuit.

items.name: mcpkit-foxpilot
items.description: FoxPilot tools via mcpkit — get the list of open tabs in the user's browser, load a url in an existing browser tab, fill multiple form fields in one step, press a keyboard key on a page (e, resize the browser window that hosts a tab, emulate device conditions for a tab, scroll the page to absolute coordinates via window, upload a local file into a file <input> on a page…

items.name: confluence-wiki
items.description: Search, read, create, and update pages on Intuit's Confluence wiki (intuit-prod.atlassian.net). Auto-triggers on wiki, confluence, wiki page, create wiki page, search wiki, read wiki, update wiki, find on wiki, or Confluence/wiki URLs.

items.name: mcpkit-bruno-api
items.description: Bruno API client MCP - discover collections, run API requests, validate environments, generate test reports from .bru files in ~/workspace/bruno-collections

items.name: mcpkit-devportal-mcp-remote
items.description: Intuit DevPortal: asset lifecycle, service ownership, org/team lookups, infra status, knowledge graph (Cypher)

items.name: web-design-guidelines
items.description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".

items.name: commerce-datafix
items.description: Use when helping authors create or review PR-driven commerce data fixes in the shopping-cart/commerce-datafix GitHub Enterprise repo for shopping carts, bulk carts, bulk cart line items, individual orders, bulk orders, or bulk order line items. Covers fix.yaml/transform.py authoring, dynamic entity lookup from GitHub references, PR workflow, Jenkins dry-run/approval/execute expectations, and rollback basics.

items.name: claude-mcp-to-codex
items.description: Translate a project's Claude Code `.mcp.json` MCP setup into Codex CLI's repo-local `.codex/config.toml` and verify it. Use when asked to copy, mirror, convert, or migrate Claude MCP config to Codex, especially for project-level setup instead of global `~/.codex/config.toml`.

items.name: git-workflow
items.description: Manages git branches, commits, PRs, and Jira tickets. Auto-triggers when user mentions branch, commit, PR, pull request, push, merge, Jira ticket, CART-, create ticket, create story, create task, create bug, story points, new branch, git checkout -b, feature branch, fix branch, bugfix, add to sprint, current sprint. Enforces CART-XXXX naming format for work projects.

items.name: intuit-idps
items.description: This project uses the centralized enterprise secrets, keys, and encryption capability (IDPS) for secret storage, key management, and encryption. Implement all secrets, encryption keys, encrypt/decrypt, key rotation, HMAC/signing, and data-protection integration using this capability; consult this skill for the required patterns and references.

items.name: video-slideshow
items.description: Creates video slideshows from images using FFmpeg. Use when user mentions slideshow, video from images, promo video, Instagram reel, social media video, marketing video, or screenshot slideshow.

items.name: ips-search
items.description: Provides guidance for IPS Search (OpenSearch-based full-text and structured search): use cases, Sidecar XML configuration, Data Change Eventing integration, onboarding steps, and support channels. IPS Search is deployed as a read replica over NoSQL or Relational data.

items.name: mcpkit-databricks-mcp
items.description: Query Databricks SQL warehouses, execute SQL, get user info, manage catalogs/schemas/tables - uses OAuth browser authentication

items.name: mcpkit-browser-relay
items.description: Browser-relay tools via mcpkit — web search via the user's browser, load a url in the browser and return its clean readable main content as {status, title, text,, search, then fetch the full readable text of the top-k results in parallel, resume a search/fetch that paused for human action (captcha or login), check relay + browser-extension connectivity and queue depth. Use this when you need to work with browser-relay.

items.name: mcpkit-cloudinary
items.description: MCP server for Cloudinary upload functionality via mcpkit — upload media (images/videos) to cloudinary. Use this when you need to work with upload.

items.name: vercel-cli-with-tokens
items.description: Deploy and manage projects on Vercel using token-based authentication. Use when working with Vercel CLI using access tokens rather than interactive login — e.g. "deploy to vercel", "set up vercel", "add environment variables to vercel".

items.name: eslint-hook-setup
items.description: Set up a Claude Code PostToolUse hook that auto-runs ESLint --fix after every file edit in a JS/TS project. One-time per-project setup. Invoke via /eslint-hook-setup.

items.name: mcpkit-slack
items.description: Slack tools via mcpkit — sends a message to a slack channel or user, schedules a message for future delivery to a slack channel, creates a slack canvas document from markdown content, updates a slack canvas with markdown content, searches for messages, files in public slack channels only, searches for messages, files in all slack channels, including public channels, private channels, dms, and group dms. Use this when you need to work with slack.

items.name: vercel-react-view-transitions
items.description: Guide for implementing smooth, native-feeling animations using React's View Transition API (`<ViewTransition>` component, `addTransitionType`, and CSS view transition pseudo-elements). Use this skill whenever the user wants to add page transitions, animate route changes, create shared element animations, animate enter/exit of components, animate list reorder, implement directional (forward/back) navigation animations, or integrate view transitions in Next.js. Also use when the user mentions view transitions, `startViewTransition`, `ViewTransition`, transition types, or asks about animating between UI states in React without third-party animation libraries.

items.name: mcpkit-cli
items.description: Use this to operate the mcpkit CLI itself — add/install new MCP servers as agent skills, call MCP tools from Bash, run servers as persistent background daemons, and manage (list, view, edit, sync, update, remove, auth) registered servers. Use whenever the user wants to add a new MCP server, wire up an MCP tool, or manage existing mcpkit servers.

items.name: interactive-explainer
items.description: Generate a self-contained, animated, interactive HTML page to explain a flow, system, algorithm, code path, state machine, pipeline, or architecture — for teaching, docs, demos, or design review. Hand-written HTML + CSS + vanilla JS + inline SVG (NO frameworks, NO build step, NO libraries beyond optional CDN fonts). Auto-triggers on phrases like "make this interactive", "animate this flow", "interactive diagram/explainer/visualization", "playable simulator", "step-through of <X>", "visualize how <X> works", "build an HTML page that explains <X>", "walkthrough page", or after writing a design/architecture doc when the user wants a visual companion. Distinct from the visual-explainer plugin skill — this is the hand-rolled, animation-first variant with no plugin dependency. Pair with intuit-pages-publish (work) or deploy-to-vercel (personal) to host the result.

items.name: mcpkit-nosql-remote-prd
items.description: Intuit IPS NoSQL data persistence (production) - entity CRUD, batch ops, relationships, queries

items.name: mcpkit-chrome-devtools
items.description: MCP server for Chrome DevTools via mcpkit — clicks on the provided element, closes the page by its index, drag an element onto another element, emulates various features on the selected page, evaluate a javascript function inside the currently selected page, type text into a input, text area or select an option from a <select> element. Use this when you need to work with chrome_devtools.

items.name: mcpkit-dast-control
items.description: Intuit DAST orchestrator admin endpoint: auth self-heal (dast_auth_status, dast_login) AND Intuit MCP management (dast_mcp_list/add/remove/edit/start/stop/enable_tool/disable_tool). For Intuit-internal MCPs on the orchestrator only; non-Intuit MCPs go to mcpkit/Claude directly.

items.name: mcpkit-ticktick
items.description: Get tools via mcpkit — get all projects from ticktick, get details about a specific project, get all tasks in a specific project, get details about a specific task, create a new task in ticktick, update an existing task in ticktick. Use this when you need to work with get.

items.name: mcpkit-subscription-mcp
items.description: Intuit subscription/billing management - subscription lifecycle, plans, billing operations

items.description: Publish a self-contained HTML page (visual explainer, interactive diagram, implementation plan, dashboard) to demo's personal Intuit GHE Pages repo (github.intuit.com/demo/diagrams) for a shareable internal link. Auto-triggers when the user wants to publish/deploy/share/host an HTML page in a WORK/Intuit context — phrases like "publish to intuit pages", "push this to demo/diagrams", "deploy this html", "make this shareable", "host on github intuit pages", "share this explainer", "give me a link for this", or after generating an HTML page from visual-explainer / generate-web-diagram / generate-slides. ONLY for work/Intuit projects — refuses for personal projects (~/personal/*).

items.name: youtube-notes
items.description: Fetches a YouTube video transcript and creates a structured Obsidian note. Use when user shares a YouTube URL or asks to take notes from a video. Triggers on YouTube links, "youtube notes", "video notes", "notes from video", "summarize video", "transcript".

items.name: browser-mcp-setup
items.description: One-time per-project setup for Chrome DevTools MCP. Creates .mcp.json, updates CLAUDE.md, and configures .gitignore. Use when a project needs browser testing but doesn't have chrome-devtools MCP configured yet. After setup, use mcp__chrome-devtools__* tools directly. Invoke via /browser-mcp-setup.

items.name: mcpkit-ixp-mcp-server
items.description: Intuit IXP (Experimentation Platform) - feature flags, experiments, A/B testing, treatment assignment

items.name: mcpkit-reddit
items.description: Clean, LLM-optimized Reddit MCP server. Browse posts, search content, analyze users. No fluff, just Reddit data. via mcpkit — fetch posts from a subreddit sorted by your choice (hot/new/top/rising), search for posts across reddit or specific subreddits, fetch a reddit post with its comments, analyze a reddit user's posting history, karma, and activity patterns, get explanations of reddit terms, slang, and culture. Use this when you need to work with reddit-mcp-buddy.

items.name: order-search
items.description: Query the IPS Order Search index (OpenSearch) for individual orders. Covers searchable fields, query patterns, aggregations, auth setup, and ready-to-run scripts for fetching real prod data.

items.name: storm-article
items.description: Generate a long-form, cited Wikipedia-style article on any topic using Stanford STORM, powered by the local Intuit Opus 4.8 proxy. Auto-triggers when the user says "storm", "write an article on", "research article", "generate a wiki article", "deep research on <topic>", "STORM <topic>", or asks for a sourced/long-form report on a subject.

items.name: intuit-mcp-orchestrator-discovery
items.description: Discover and register Intuit-internal MCP servers into mcpkit by talking directly to the local IntuitDev MCP Orchestrator running on 127.0.0.1:53333. Use this whenever the user wants to add a new Intuit MCP (IXP, NoSQL, ACP, Glean, policy engine, or any other Intuit-internal capability) to mcpkit, asks "what other Intuit MCPs are available", wants to register a server they only know as a public https://*.api.intuit.com/... endpoint, hits an AuthenticationFailed error registering an Intuit MCP directly, or asks how dast-orch/ixp-mcp-server/nosql-remote-prd type servers were added. Also use this whenever the user gives a GitHub Enterprise (github.intuit.com) URL to a Claude/Cursor PLUGIN and wants its MCPs (and skills) added to mcpkit / the headless orchestrator — e.g. devportal-claude-plugin, or any dev-devx/*-plugin repo with a .mcp.json. Also covers registering builtin servers (transport builtin, e.g. dast-auth on the orchestrator's dedicated --auth-port) that have no per-server stream route. Also use this to check whether a specific Intuit MCP server is currently running/stopped before debugging a "connection refused" error on an Intuit MCP.

items.name: app-store-graphics
items.description: Generates detailed AI image prompts for Play Store / App Store graphics. Use when user mentions app store graphics, play store screenshots, app icon prompt, feature graphic, store listing images, marketing assets, or app screenshots.

items.description: Intuit capability discovery and recommendations - find capabilities, offerings, and code examples for Intuit platform services

items.description: Semantic search across Obsidian vault notes — find relevant notes by topic, concept, or keyword. Use when you need to search the user's knowledge base, find related notes, or look up information in Obsidian.

items.name: cloakbrowser-setup
items.description: Sets up the CloakBrowser MCP server (stealth Chromium that bypasses Cloudflare Turnstile, reCAPTCHA, FingerprintJS, BrowserScan, etc.) for the current project across Claude Code (`.mcp.json`), Augment (`.augment/settings.json`), and Codex CLI (`.codex/config.toml`) — with a project-relative persistent profile directory so cookies/sign-in survive restarts. Run this skill when the user asks to set up CloakBrowser, add stealth browser automation, scrape a site that blocks normal browsers (Cloudflare-protected, fingerprint-checked), or test a logged-in flow / Chrome extension that requires a real browser session. The skill merges into existing config files without clobbering other MCP servers, appends usage docs into the project's canonical agent instructions file (`AGENTS.md`, with `CLAUDE.md` symlinked to it), and adds the data directory to `.gitignore`.

items.name: ips-nosql
items.description: Provides full workflow coverage for IPS NoSQL Data Persistence (Cassandra/DynamoDB document store): entity schema XML authoring with PII/7216 classification, PR submission to dynamic-entities-release, QA/E2E/Performance/FMEA environment progression, production release, and Java Spring Boot SDK implementation (CRUD, batch, relationships, queries, auth, cross-region retry, anti-patterns).

items.name: argocd-k8s
items.description: Manages Argo CD deployments and Kubernetes operations at Intuit. Auto-triggers when user mentions deployment, deploy, argocd, argo, kubernetes, k8s, pods, rollout, sync, health status, degraded, progressing, suspended, promotion, rollback, environment status, qal, e2e, prf, stg, prd, usw2, use2, check deployment, monitor deployment, or deployment logs.

items.name: headless-orchestrator
items.description: How the standalone headless Intuit MCP orchestrator works — the always-running LaunchAgent (com.intuit.devx.headless-orchestrator) that replaces the IntuitDeveloperDesktopApp GUI and serves 127.0.0.1:53333 for all mcpkit-registered Intuit MCPs (ixp-mcp-server, nosql-remote-prd, subscription-mcp, dev-assist-remote-mcp, etc.). Use this whenever an Intuit MCP returns AuthenticationFailed / 401 / "connection refused" on 53333, when the user asks how the headless orchestrator authenticates or refreshes tokens, when auth needs a fresh device-code login (invalid_grant / refresh token expired), when the service needs restarting or status-checking, or when rebuilding the binary after pulling new source. It self-refreshes via a background scheduler (replacing the GUI's manual "refresh" button) and exposes dast_auth_status / dast_login MCP tools on /mcp/stream so an MCP client can inspect auth state and trigger an in-band re-login.

items.name: agent-browser
items.description: FALLBACK browser automation CLI. Use ONLY when Chrome DevTools MCP (browser-mcp-setup) cannot handle the task -- e.g., multi-page workflows with complex navigation, form automation with file uploads/drag-and-drop, tasks needing persistent browser state across sessions, or when Chrome DevTools MCP errors out. For standard browser testing, visual verification, and UI inspection, use browser-mcp-setup first.

items.name: hoppscotch-to-bruno
items.description: Convert Hoppscotch API collection JSON exports to Bruno format and import into Bruno app. Auto-triggers when user mentions hoppscotch, hopscotch, convert to bruno, import to bruno, hoppscotch export, api collection convert, hopp to bruno, migrate from hoppscotch.

items.name: intuit-docker-registry
items.description: Intuit Docker/Artifactory registry knowledge - authentication, building, pushing images, registry endpoints, troubleshooting. items.name: git-workflow
items.description: Manages git branches, commits, PRs, and Jira tickets. Auto-triggers when user mentions branch, commit, PR, pull request, push, merge, Jira ticket, CART-, create ticket, create story, create task, create bug, story points, new branch, git checkout -b, feature branch, fix branch, bugfix, add to sprint, current sprint. Enforces CART-XXXX naming format for work projects.

items.name: intuit-idps
items.description: This project uses the centralized enterprise secrets, keys, and encryption capability (IDPS) for secret storage, key management, and encryption. Implement all secrets, encryption keys, encrypt/decrypt, key rotation, HMAC/signing, and data-protection integration using this capability; consult this skill for the required patterns and references.

items.name: video-slideshow

items.description: Provides guidance for IPS Search (OpenSearch-based full-text and structured search): use cases, Sidecar XML configuration, Data Change Eventing integration, onboarding steps, and support channels. IPS Search is deployed as a read replica over NoSQL or Relational data.

Use this when you need to work with browser-relay.

items.name: eslint-hook-setup
items.description: Set up a Claude Code PostToolUse hook that auto-runs ESLint --fix after every file edit in a JS/TS project. One-time per-project setup. Invoke via /eslint-hook-setup.

items.name: mcpkit-slack
items.description: Slack tools via mcpkit — sends a message to a slack channel or user, schedules a message for future delivery to a slack channel, creates a slack canvas document from markdown content, updates a slack canvas with markdown content, searches for messages, files in public slack channels only, searches for messages, files in all slack channels, including public channels, private channels, dms, and group dms. Use this when you need to work with slack.

items.name: vercel-react-view-transitions
items.description: Guide for implementing smooth, native-feeling animations using React's View Transition API (`<ViewTransition>` component, `addTransitionType`, and CSS view transition pseudo-elements). Use this skill whenever the user wants to add page transitions, animate route changes, create shared element animations, animate enter/exit of components, animate list reorder, implement directional (forward/back) navigation animations, or integrate view transitions in Next.js. Also use when the user mentions view transitions, `startViewTransition`, `ViewTransition`, transition types, or asks about animating between UI states in React without third-party animation libraries.

items.name: mcpkit-cli
items.description: Use this to operate the mcpkit CLI itself — add/install new MCP servers as agent skills, call MCP tools from Bash, run servers as persistent background daemons, and manage (list, view, edit, sync, update, remove, auth) registered servers. Use whenever the user wants to add a new MCP server, wire up an MCP tool, or manage existing mcpkit servers.

items.name: interactive-explainer
items.description: Generate a self-contained, animated, interactive HTML page to explain a flow, system, algorithm, code path, state machine, pipeline, or architecture — for teaching, docs, demos, or design review. Hand-written HTML + CSS + vanilla JS + inline SVG (NO frameworks, NO build step, NO libraries beyond optional CDN fonts). Auto-triggers on phrases like "make this interactive", "animate this flow", "interactive diagram/explainer/visualization", "playable simulator", "step-through of <X>", "visualize how <X> works", "build an HTML page that explains <X>", "walkthrough page", or after writing a design/architecture doc when the user wants a visual companion. Distinct from the visual-explainer plugin skill — this is the hand-rolled, animation-first variant with no plugin dependency. Pair with intuit-pages-publish (work) or deploy-to-vercel (personal) to host the result.

Use this when you need to work with reddit-mcp-buddy.

Auto-triggers when the user says "storm", "write an article on", "research article", "generate a wiki article", "deep research on <topic>", "STORM <topic>", or asks for a sourced/long-form report on a subject.

items.name: intuit-mcp-orchestrator-discovery
items.description: Discover and register Intuit-internal MCP servers into mcpkit by talking directly to the local IntuitDev MCP Orchestrator running on 127.0.0.1:53333. Use this whenever the user wants to add a new Intuit MCP (IXP, NoSQL, ACP, Glean, policy engine, or any other Intuit-internal capability) to mcpkit, asks "what other Intuit MCPs are available", wants to register a server they only know as a public https://*.api.intuit.com/... endpoint, hits an AuthenticationFailed error registering an Intuit MCP directly, or asks how dast-orch/ixp-mcp-server/nosql-remote-prd type servers were added. Also use this whenever the user gives a GitHub Enterprise (github.intuit.com) URL to a Claude/Cursor PLUGIN and wants its MCPs (and skills) added to mcpkit / the headless orchestrator — e.g. devportal-claude-plugin, or any dev-devx/*-plugin repo with a .mcp.json. Also covers registering builtin servers (transport builtin, e.g. dast-auth on the orchestrator's dedicated --auth-port) that have no per-server stream route. Also use this to check whether a specific Intuit MCP server is currently running/stopped before debugging a "connection refused" error on an Intuit MCP.

items.name: cloakbrowser-setup
items.description: Sets up the CloakBrowser MCP server (stealth Chromium that bypasses Cloudflare Turnstile, reCAPTCHA, FingerprintJS, BrowserScan, etc.) for the current project across Claude Code (`.mcp.json`), Augment (`.augment/settings.json`), and Codex CLI (`.codex/config.toml`) — with a project-relative persistent profile directory so cookies/sign-in survive restarts. Run this skill when the user asks to set up CloakBrowser, add stealth browser automation, scrape a site that blocks normal browsers (Cloudflare-protected, fingerprint-checked), or test a logged-in flow / Chrome extension that requires a real browser session. The skill merges into existing config files without clobbering other MCP servers, appends usage docs into the project's canonical agent instructions file (`AGENTS.md`, with `CLAUDE.md` symlinked to it), and adds the data directory to `.gitignore`.

items.name: bulk-shopping-mcp
items.description: Drive the bulk-shopping-ai-agent MCP server to execute bulk QuickBooks subscription purchases for accountants and firms. Use when the calling AI needs to perform a bulk shopping flow, place a bulk order, run an accountant cart for multiple clients (existing or NTTF), or run a midmarket quote / wholesale subscription flow with firm parent-child linking.

items.name: .env user ticket hygiene for E2E tests
items.description: Do not persist user_id/token pairs in .env for bulk-shopping-ai-agent E2E tests — let the test resolve them fresh

items.name: Splunk REST Instances
items.description: Splunk REST API endpoints, credentials, and which instance has which logs (eval pipeline logs are on IP, service logs on T4i)

items.name: feedback-e2e-replay-machine-sleep
items.description: Long unattended E2E replay runs get wrecked by macOS sleep — process suspends, downstream calls hit AWS-ELB 403; use caffeinate and keep runs short

items.name: Default to parallel subagents for independent investigation prongs
items.description: When investigations have independent reads, spawn parallel Explore agents in one message instead of doing them sequentially in the main session

items.name: genos-eval-metrics-retrieval-run-knobs
items.description: How to fetch eval metric values (genosevalsvc runs API), why they're not in Splunk, and the eval_config/wide-window knobs for the genosevalsvc trigger

items.name: eval-auth-pattern-no-idps-for-llm-auth
items.description: Eval pipeline uses GenOS test client creds directly for LLM auth (no IDPS). IDPS only needed for Langfuse keys. Reference impl is agent-eval-utils.

items.name: Confluence CLI: route via Intuit api-proxy, not direct atlassian.net
items.description: When confluence-cli returns 403 "IP not in allowlist", the cause is IP allowlist on intuit-prod.atlassian.net — fix is to point at atlassian.api-proxy.intuit.com (same proxy jira-cli uses)

items.name: verify-nosql-schema
items.description: Probe the IPS NoSQL endpoint to confirm that newly-added schema attributes and indexes are live in a given env (QA-SHR, MSE-uswest2, etc.) after a `dynamic-entities-release` deploy. Use when the user asks to "verify schema deployment", "check QA NoSQL schema", "confirm new attrs are live", "test schema round-trip", "did the schema PR deploy", or mentions a `QA-Ready` / `QA-Deployed` / `FMEA` / production NoSQL promotion that we want to validate.

items.name: Direct commits on deploy branches — no PR ceremony

items.name: Always invoke git-workflow skill for git operations
items.description: Never bypass the git-workflow skill for branches, commits, PRs — it handles Jira ticket creation/linking automatically

items.name: Never clobber working-tree changes with git checkout
items.description: Refuse to run `git checkout <ref> -- file` against files that have uncommitted working-tree changes — stash or diff-apply instead.
